title: How Facebook's Algorithmic Tweaks Affected Engagement with News URLs over Time
snippet: A media blog recap of the 2026 International Communication Association (ICA) conference in Cape Town, detailing Fabio Giglietto's expert presentation. The piece covers Giglietto's extensive analysis on how Facebook's changing algorithmic governance and user political alignment have impacted the reach and amplification of news URLs over a six-year period.
description: A detailed blog summary of Fabio Giglietto's co-authored paper on Facebook's algorithmic amplification and news engagement, presented at the 2026 ICA conference.

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: This news article highlights research co-authored by Fabio Giglietto showing that Meta's political content reduction filters disproportionately reduced the reach of mainstream politicians (-43% on average) while allowing political extremist pages to flourish by increasing their posting frequency.
description: An Italian news article highlighting research co-authored by Fabio Giglietto on how Facebook's algorithms affect the reach of political parties and extremist content.

title: Panel – Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research? – #SMSociety 2026
snippet: A conference speaker announcement indicating Fabio Giglietto's participation as a panelist at the International Conference on Social Media & Society (#SMSociety 2026) in Glasgow. The roundtable focuses on academic researchers navigating, sharing code, and finding workarounds for the systemic flaws of the Meta Content Library (MCL).
description: Fabio Giglietto is featured as a panelist for the Meta Content Library Community Roundtable at the International Conference on Social Media & Society, discussing methodologies and challenges in academic social media research.

title: Indagine urbinate: "Così l’algoritmo influenza il voto" - Il Resto del Carlino
description: News article from Il Resto del Carlino

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: This article discusses the political and institutional impact of Professor Giglietto's study on Meta's algorithmic deprioritization of political content. It notes that his research findings sparked a European Parliament inquiry, a European Commission investigation under the Digital Services Act, and a formal hearing request by AGCOM (Italy's communications regulator).
description: An article discussing the political and public fallout of Professor Giglietto's research on Meta's algorithms, including its feature on Rai3's 'Report' and subsequent European Parliament inquiries.
